source_pr.title: planner: stable the test introduced by #18474
source_pr.body:
### What problem does this PR solve?

Problem Summary: Reported by @wjhuang2016 , it seems that 18784 introduced an unstable test. The error from jenkin/unit-test/47817 comes from the original test `TestValidator`. And I only did some code refactor/function encapsulation. The only logic difference is that I moved a defered function to `TearDownTest`.

I can not reproduce the error. But my guess is that the dom loop goroutine is not disabled due to the lack of `s.dom.Close()`.

### Check List

Tests

- Unit test
- Integration test

### Release note

- No release note

symptoms: Leak/cleanup related flakes in preprocess tests (e.g., domain/store background goroutines not closed, causing `testleak` failures).
failure_signature: `testleak` reports leaked goroutines due to missing/late `s.dom.Close()` / `s.store.Close()` in preprocess tests
root_cause_explanation: The test suite creates a domain/store that spawns background goroutines. When `s.dom.Close()` / `s.store.Close()` are not reliably executed for each test, leaked goroutines can be detected by `testleak` and cause intermittent CI failures. This PR stabilizes the tests by explicitly deferring `Close()` of `dom` and `store` inside each affected test (`TestValidator`, `TestForeignKey`) so cleanup is guaranteed even when test flow changes.
fix_pattern: Ensure per-test cleanup of domain/store resources (defer `Close()` inside each test) to prevent goroutine leaks and `testleak` flakes.

evidence: evidence: PR title: stable the test introduced by #18474.
evidence: updates preprocess tests to remove nondeterministic behavior introduced earlier (planner/core/preprocess_test.go).
